title: Level 626
wikitext:
{{CSS|User:Terraria is good... for reasons./Coloranthologypurple.css}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Funnel+Sans:ital,wght@0,300..800;1,300..800&display=swap'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Funnel Sans'; } }}{{Background|file=Level 626.webp}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 626: "Forest of Psychosis"}}{{M.E.G Notice|notice=Please wear a gas mask or bring an abundant supply of Almond Water.|reason=This level produces copious amounts of purple gas that can cause one to hallucinate constantly and can cause mild headaches. Breathing it in alone can cause one to suffer from it's symptoms, with the only viable options being Almond Water or using other protective gear such as a gas mask.}}{{Class Ψ}}Level 626 is the 627th level of the Backrooms. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Level 626.webp|caption=An image of Level 626.|width=420px}} Level 626, informally known as "Forest of Psychosis" or simply "Psychosis Forest" is a sprawling, dense purple rainforest that is estimated to extend approximately 20 million mi². The forest is made up of tall trees that span from 20 to 130 feet high; the tree types found in this level consist of pine, birch, oak, and fir trees. Notably, most of Level 626's trees are fire-resistant and are practically indestructible, although some speculate that powerful entities may be able to destroy them.A few reports have noted that some trees have been split apart, presumably by either a powerful entity or through other means. Attempts at destroying the trees have resulted in equipment shattering into pieces on impact. However, this does not apply to fallen trees and logs.Testing on fallen trees or logs has shown productive results, with said fallen trees or logs losing their indestructibility. Aside from trees, other natural terrain is also present, including rocks, fallen trees, rivers, potholes and lakes. Man-made structures are also present, including abandoned cabins, watchtowers, tunnels, piles of logs and fungi farms, indicating previous human habitation. Unique lakes, rivers and ponds dot the level, with purple water being the most abundant. While the water itself is drinkable, it is not entirely clean, and may cause one to contract several infections without proper sanitation. Additionally, certain bodies of water possess an anomaly. If such water comes into contact with a wanderer, it exerts an attractive force, suspected to be gravitational in nature, that can pull wanderers in, leading to drowning. Such pools are dubbed sinking water. Hallucinations are a common cause of many sinking water victims, so it is recommended to stay away from these bodies of water altogether. There is no known way to differentiate regular and sinking water, making it more dangerous. Due to this, it is recommended to avoid lakes and rivers altogether. Overall, the plant life within Level 626 is hazardous, as most of the greenery periodically release thick purple gas throughout the level, causing mild headaches and minor hallucinations to wanderers. The hallucinations usually appear as visions, mostly consisting of dangerous entities, objects that are out of place, or even visions of themselves falling to the [[Phenomenon 35|wretched cycle]]. Luckily, one may negate the hallucinations by drinking [[Object 1|Almond Water]], although finding Almond Water is quite difficult. Furthermore, certain entities such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]] are unaffected by this phenomenon. Artificial objects can also be found throughout the level, usually held in random crates that contain various items. These include but are not limited to wooden logs, plastic bottles, and harmful items such as Liquid Pain. Though beneficial objects can also be found in these crates, usually consisting of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], tools, weapons and first-aid kits, all of these are rare and difficult to come across in '''Level 626.''' It is recommended to find man made structures, as objects are mostly abundant within the abandoned cabins and fungi farms. Anomalous areas known as "gravity zones" sparsely populate the level. These zones have significantly altered gravity compared to most of the level, which can be either stronger or weaker than normal. Entering gravity zones is potentially hazardous due to movement in these zones being much harder to control. Furthermore, gravity zones have only been recorded to affect wanderers; not entities, objects and/or materials. Some wanderers have found success in using their gear to control their movement due to the gravity not affecting items, but this can be rather difficult. Luckily, gravity zones are more desaturated in color compared to the surrounding environment, making them easy to spot. == ''Psychomitus insaniae'' == {{Blurimage|file=Psychomitus Insaniae.jpg|caption=An Insanity Shroom.|width=320px}} The level is well-known for its [[Object 10|Backshrooms]], due to their abundance. They mostly appear around wet areas, such as rivers, lakes and even trees. Furthermore, Backshrooms appear in larger clusters around wet areas and vice versa. Due to this, many outposts are settled in this level, despite psychological hazards. A special type of Backshroom, colloquially known as Insanity Shrooms (''P. insaniae'') is a volatile mushroom that mostly appear as small candy-like mushrooms, packed into clusters. Ingesting these will cause extreme hallucinations, nausea, loss of energy, a feeling of helplessness or fear, and long-lasting insanity. Symptoms usually last about 2-8 hours, with the only exception being the long-lasting insanity, which lasts for 1-2 days. Insanity Shrooms are only found in natural levels with psychological hazards. They are by far most abundant in this level. In addition to the harmful effects of ingestion, these Shrooms release a strange gas when damaged or burst. The gas, which is different from the purple gas released by the rest of the level's flora, causes one to slowly go insane if inhaled in sufficient amounts. It is unknown whether the gas affects entities. Due to these factors, Insanity Shrooms are useful for fighting other colonies/outposts as they inflict psychological harm to the victim. == Additional Information == === Level 626 Origins === Theories suggest that Level 626 originally was part of the [[Level 630/offset|pre-contamination]] version of [[Level 630]] before The Split. It is theorized at one point, the level was broken into 2 similar levels and drifted apart across [[The Void]]. Soon after The Split, the environment of Level 626 was drastically changed, acquiring the psychological hazards it now possesses. The phenomena and/or events that caused these changes are currently unknown. These changes did not just affect the landscape but also affected the flora and fauna, changing the nature of the area altogether. The area underwent severe mutations and expansions, taking on a completely new form that bore little resemblance to its original state, other than remaining a forest. The aftermath made the forest dangerous and near-uninhabitable, due to the new psychological hazards being present in this level and a sudden increase of dangerous entities in this level. === A Note === A random note was found placed on a tree after one of the surviving M.E.G members fled from Outpost 626, presumably taken by said member while fleeing. The words were written in an unknown purple substance, and are as follows:
Day 4: I HEAR THEM I HEAR THEM BREATHE, I SEE THEM WALKING ALONGSIDE ME. THEY ARE EVERYWHERE, ALL AROUND ME, BESIDE AND ABOVE. I CAN HEAR THEM SPEAK IN MANY DIFFERENT LANGUAGES, I CAN TASTE THEIR SORROWS, THEIR CONSTANT TALKING RHYTHYMS. I AM THEM, AND THEY ARE ME. FOR THEM, I SHALL WALK INTO THE PURPLE MIST, INTO THE PURPLE WATER, TO JOIN THEM. INHALE THE PURPLE GAS, AND JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US. JOIN US.
It is presumed the note was written by a wanderer under the hallucinogenic effects of the purple gas. Their whereabouts are currently unknown. === Temperature and Climate === Level 626's temperature is inconsistent; the temperature at daytime ranges from 15°C to 30°C, and at nighttime the temperature ranges from -15°C to 10°C. Regardless of the temperature, snow and ice are absent from the level. The day-night cycle of the level takes approximately 16-24 hoursThe length of the day-night cycle, as well as that of each part of the cycle, varies every day. to complete, leading to 8-12 hours of sunlight and 8-12 hours of darkness. Similarly to [[Level 351]], the moon in the level always appears to be completely red, and will always be clearly visible through the level at night. There have been reports of the level having multiple moons; however, these reports are most likely caused by wanderers' hallucinations. The level’s weather is mostly clear, though it also includes other occasional weathers such as rain and purple fog. == Entities == The only entities that are found in this level are [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]] and Wretches. It is presumed that they are more hostile than in other levels due to the unknown effects of the purple gas. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the abundance of Backshrooms in this level, many groups have settled in this level, despite the psychological hazards. Currently, there are three recorded large outposts within '''Level 626'''. The twelve minor outposts that reside in this level have been omitted for the sake of brevity. === M.E.G Outpost 626 === * An outpost created by the [[M.E.G.]] to gather/grow Backshrooms, research the level and establish a significant foothold within '''Level 626'''; * Has several research bases and fungi farms throughout the level; * The group has a current member count of approximately 140, consisting of various divisions of farmers, researchers, guards, and explorers; * They are friendly and welcoming towards anyone who enters in contact with them, unless provoked; * Known for maintaining decent relations with most groups, and mostly trades with other groups for supplies; * Has a strong hostility to the Republic Of Aurielle, due them involving in various conflicts and wars; * Was destroyed during The War Of Level 626 by The Republic Of Aurielle, the remaining surviving M.E.G members fled to other levels. === Republic Of Aurielle Fungi Base === * A base created by the Republic of Aurielle to gather Backshrooms and maintain their influence in Level 626; * Has several man-made cabins, fungi farms, and research bases to house citizens and natives; * The group includes 200 residents, where almost all of them are natives; * Are relatively friendly to non-affiliated wanderers and allied groups, however they are hostile to enemies such as the [[M.E.G.]]; * Are only open for trading to allied groups. === Red Cross Base Psychosis === * A base created by the [[Backrooms Red Cross]] focused primarily on finding new medical substances and researching the environment of the level, along with maintaining and providing for the health of wanderers who pass by; * The group has approximately 70 members, each having their own role in the organization; * It assists vulnerable wanderers in situations of disaster/crisis by contributing in averting and alleviating suffering in all its forms, protecting health and mental wellbeing, along with cultivating respect toward human dignity; * The group has a decently large amount of food, medical supplies and other necessary resources in order to help wanderers survive; * Known to have good relations with other outposts in this level, and aid struggling groups; * They are friendly and open to trade for anyone who enters in contact with them. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * At [[Level 416]], noclipping through a rare purple tree will send one here. * Walking by a forest during the violet rain at [[Level 626.2]] will send one here. * Climbing a rare ladder at [[Level 483]] will send the wanderer to one of the cabins in this level. * Natives in [[Level 702]] may sometimes send one here instead of [[The Void]] if one commits major offenses throughout the level. === Exits === * One may exit to [[Level 483]] by finding a trap door in the basement of some cabins and climbing down the ladder set in the trapdoor. However, cabins that include a ladder are uncommon. * Walking too far along a purple cloverfield path may lead to [[Level 626.2]]. * Finding an extremely rare passage and walking towards it may lead to [[Level 630]]. == Addendum == {{Login|organization=M.E.G.|username={{USERNAME}}|password=nememeyanenenemnememeyanenenemnememeyanenenemnememeyanenenemnememeyanenenem|body=
Addendum: "They Appeared out of Nowhere"{{Blurimage|file=Level_626.jpg|caption=A piece of paper that was found on a rotten log.}}
I don't know how long I've been walking since it happened, I think about 2 hours or so? No no I can't think about that right now, gotta write, gotta write. Look..I don't know who it was that raided us, I'm guessing another colony or whatever, but in any case I'll write what I can and go. They came out of nowhere, threw the shrooms in our outpost, I don't remember much but they threw in a lot of em. I think that's when the slaughter started. I saw many get killed, right in front of my eyes, thankfully I had my sh-- straight and ran out as soon as I could.

I think..well hopefully think that some made it out, maybe fled to other levels. I'd like to think that I wasn't the unlucky one..the one that wouldn't be stranded and knew their way to Red Cross Base. I just hope that's true, cause I think I'm starting to lose it. I keep seeing things, I don't know if they're real but I keep seeing them inch closer and closer- Oh wait..I see someo[[File:Scribble.png|frameless]]

The initial writer is presumably dead.
